titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( (Winant) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 10856. Comea 134. By letter of December 4 (copy transmitted to the Department by air today)In despatch 19728, December 7, from London, not printed. the British representative on the European Advisory Commission proposed the early establishment of a committee of experts to work on “the material before the Commission relating to the disarmament and demilitarization of Germany”. As stated in an aide-mémoire of October 25 from the Soviet Embassy to the Foreign Office and as mentioned in my telegram No. 9851 (November 11, 8 p.m., Comea 126), the Soviet Government also attaches a high priority to this subject. At tonight’s meeting of the EAC it was agreed to appoint this committee tomorrow to begin its work immediately.
I have now circulated the US draft directive on elimination and prohibition of military training in Germany in accordance with Department’s instruction No. 4684 of October 28.Not printed.

The revised US draft directive on “disarmament of German armed forces and disposal of enemy equipment” which incorporates the comments of the Joint Chiefs of Staff (Department’s instructions No. 4664 of October 24 and No. 4722 of November 4)Neither printed. was transmitted with my despatch No. 19300 of November 18.Not printed. Early clearance in Washington of this draft directive is essential if the US views are to be adequately presented in the now imminent discussions on this question.

The US draft directive on “disposal of German armed forces subsequent to surrender” was transmitted with my despatch No. 16970 of July 20.Not printed. Comment and clearance on this important directive are similarly awaited urgently as a basis for stating US views and interests in the Commission’s discussions.
